# vectorized_filter { #xileh.lib.signal.filter.vectorized_filter }

```python
lib.signal.filter.vectorized_filter(x, b, a, zi)
```

The scipy filter is only able of dealing with 1d vectors
    use this wrapper to track n dimensional states via partial()
    Not really vectorized of course

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name   | Type                                         | Description                         | Default    |
|--------|----------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------|------------|
| x      | (np.array, (ntimes, nchan))                  | data matrix                         | _required_ |
| b      | np.array                                     | filter parameters b                 | _required_ |
| a      | np.array                                     | filter parameters a                 | _required_ |
| zi     | (np.array, (max(len(a), len(b)) - 1, nchan)) | filter states separate per channels | _required_ |

## Returns: {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

    fdata : np.array, (ntimes, nchan)
        the filtered data per channel
    zi : np.array, (max(len(a), len(b))-1, nchan)
        the updated filter state
